item.content:encoded: Exciting stuff! Duckbert Gottfried has always been one of my favorite articles I’ve written and I’m pleased to see him getting his dues!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*insert a Ducktales pun/allusion here*
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That contradiction is the problem I have with this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems more like a J class entry doesn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I got injured, SCP-2730 helped me with out-of-pocket expenses that my medical insurance didn't cover!

Also, +1
Good god why

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A year late, but holy fuck, headcanon'd HARD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The final interview ratchet it up to 11 for me. The mental image of the Rothfuss peeling off his face…*shivers*. Take my +1.

Would like to see what anomalous stuff Carrot Top was getting up to. (I counted the letters of the redacted name and looked up the cast of "The Aristocrats")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for body horror, the intriguing idea of the Lilith protocol and sheer wtf weirdness. But I have a very hard time believing that the Foundation would allow a 2730 instance to continue working for them when they apparently have the ability to transform people into more of themselves with nothing but a brief conversation.

Also noticed a contradiction. How come the Gottfried duck still looks like a human after the Foundation took its mask and can only be revealed by infrared cameras, but the researcher takes off his mask and the disguise is completely gone?

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Psst… I think you forgot to upvote

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Only until you can convince someone else that they're the joke. Also, +1 for making me laugh AND be slightly creeped out.
This was supposed to be a joke and now it's my third most popular article does that make me the joke

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagine Dr Bright trying to interview one of these things.

He then becomes The Duckman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck?

I'm duck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the duck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my head this is connected to the star ducks mentioned in 1952

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sanders, Lovelace, and Holmes yes. The other two I would have to look up.

I think you are missing my point though. I wasn't saying that you are deficient for not recognizing a spectacularly unfunny comic by name, I was merely suggesting that his two most prominent voice roles are likely familiar to most, regardless; even if you aren't able to connect the name with the role.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suspect those people are even less obscure. Anyway I am not trying to deny parochialism, that is inherent in any article that relies on some actual famous person and Gottfried isn't different from Duncan, LaBeouf, or Sanders in this way. just pointing out that you probably did have prior exposure to Gottfried without even knowing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your examples demonstrate the parochialism you're trying to deny. Try substituting "The Refridgerator", "Rachel Maddow" and "Don Rickles" for "Gilbert Gottfried" to see why.

Imagine the following SCP items:

  • Garden mulch that thinks it's Larry Sanders
  • The Scott Flansburg calculator
  • Ada Lovelace, reincarnated as a sentient computer language
  • Mr Robert Dobbalina, who exists only on airport tannoys
  • H&H Homes, an estate agent for cannibal houses

Do you get all these references?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to re-read it a moment again to see what was what. There are a few questions that I am left with from reading this. Now… I agree that not every hole has to be plugged and every possible loose end has to be tied, but there are some things I would want to find out if I were reading this from an 'in the Foundation' perspective.

But if I understand it correctly there is a mask with the DNA of Gilbert in storage.
This mask is harvested for DNA to create clones of Gilbert to replace the otherwise anomalous Gilbert with, until such a time it dies in a public non-natural manner (or it's just 'time to retire the persona' if he gets ~too~ old).
The 2730 that was a duck but is now a man is still in containment? How did they get the mask off to take DNA from if it's not a mask anymore?
The 2730 that was a man but is now a duck still gets to pretend to be a man and is now a special-class 'employee'. But why haven't they attempted to 'normalize' their researcher again by reapplying the same rhetoric that Gilbert Duckfried back at it and make it the SCP again?

In the end though, I was left with the following echoing in my head.
"I heard it's a famous actor and comedian!"
"I heard it's actually a brilliant researcher!"
"I heard it's a giant duck!"

(Still keeping my +1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most people who are reading fiction websites in English have probably seen either Disney's Aladin, an Alflac commercial or both…

item.title: USA UP ALL NIGHT
item.content:encoded:
Sure if you are around the upper age limit of higher thirties the you remember USA up all night with troma classics and bad horror. Rhonda hosted Saturdays and glibert hosted Fridays. And that's how we got to know this guy.

This SCP is so meta. Especially the flip flop. I normally don't like short scps. But this is perfect..

I'm Gilbert godfried and I'm not the duck, you're the duck. Holy fuck.

Very enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not everyone in the world knows who Gilbert Gottfried is. I had to google him, and I'm not sure I'm glad I did. So, some people in the Foundation will need it explaining - just in a footnote or subordinate clause.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot I already wrote a review, lmao.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this some sort of virus, or an mass infiltration, or the truth behind humanity: shape-shifting humanoid ducks? No need for an explanation; it's weird.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never liked the idea behind this, and honestly, I still don't.

At this moment, the camera feed is disrupted for two seconds. When the feed resumes, the infrared feed shows SCP-2730 as Gilbert Gottfried, while Rothfuss appears to be an instance of SCP-2730. SCP-2730 turns the laptop back towards Rothfuss so he can see the feed.

But holy shit does this work as a great piece of SCP-style horror writing. And now I'm wondering things like what are they doing with the original instance? If it shows up at Gottfried in every way, does that mean he's "cured" and back to being himself? So +1 from me, and no regrets.

item.title: +50 in 20 hours
item.content:encoded: This is truly the death of comedy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was anyone else confused when they thought it was implying Gilbert Gottfried was in the Aristocats?

No images in the forums!
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You just made it ten times better, and more creepy too!

1+

item.title: Re: This is too long
item.content:encoded:
That's what I mean. That's why I said he rated it unfairly.

But thank you for telling me anyway.

item.title: Re: This is too long
item.content:encoded: Actually, it's perfectly fine to downvote something for being long if it fails to hold your interest throughout. The problem here is that this article isn't that long, and the feedback given by this user is quite unhelpful.

item.title: Re: This is too long
item.content:encoded: You sir, are damn mighty correct in that!

item.title: Re: This is too long
item.content:encoded:
Did you really downvote something because TL;DR?

Like… I don't think that's a good reason to downvote something.

Plus 173 is more simple then Gilbert duck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like Gilbert Gottfried himself, I originally found this mildly annoying but now find it horrifying.

item.title: Re: This is too long
item.content:encoded: SCP-173 is the first SCP, it has nothing to tell other than that it is a monster. This scip, however, has something to tell and a feeling to give the reader. Length does not dictate quality. There are stories that have to be huge to tell the story they want to tell. It seems kinda unfair to rate an article on how long it is instead of the quality of the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, God, it's back.

And now it's actually kind of compelling. Take your +1, you maniac.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gives new meaning to the game "duck, duck, Goose."

item.title: This is too long
item.content:encoded: Staff Notice: this user is permanently banned for plagiarism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I loved this one the first time around, but damn did the new version blow the old one out of the water. My only question is, since the Foundation is now aware of these biological anomalies disguised as humans, why aren't they actively investigating the rest of their staff to determine if they are also instances of 2730?

Anyway, a hearty +1 for what is easily one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No son… you are the ducks!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus Christ, how horrifying.

I dig this skip. Not only does this article do a good job of turning the fact that Gilbert Godfried has a duck voice into something really weird and creepy, the realization that there may be many, many more uncontained instances of 2730 out and about adds a good amount of fridge horror. Add the fact that the article is brief enough to not overstay its welcome, and you have a winner.

+1 ARD. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear God….

Upvoted with all my might

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the ever-loving hell? Pass me whatever it is you're on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I'm worried I'm going to be turned into a duck but still think I'm papachan. Thanks for the nightmares.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Existential dread and body horror got drunk and made a baby. An ANOMALOUS baby, that looks like a duck and thinks it's Gilbert Gottfried.

This is right up there with the James Bond beehive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: J'duccuse!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We're all ducks on the inside.

+1 quack

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once again, upvoted out of sheer Dada fascination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Fuck, it got weirder.

…

+1, ya filthy animal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: take my upvote once more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rothfuss: Nothing! I am Edward Rothfuss!

SCP-2730: How are you able to make yourself look like Gilbert Gottfried?

Rothfuss: Because I am Edward Rothfuss!

Did you mean:

"SCP-2730: How are you able to make yourself look like Edward Rothfuss?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you improved your abstract grindhouse shitposting skills

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much more improved in terms of storyline.

item.title: Not sure if Cinderella or Limp Bizkit
item.content:encoded:
Not much to say about this one. Consider it an experimental post-modern arthouse project.

See more of my work on my author page!
